core_mechanism: a critical-threshold instability in which the relative counter-motion of two interpenetrating coupled components destabilizes above a critical driving flux into a self-sustained disordered (turbulent) state maintained by the coupling between the components
brief_reason: The abstract states that above a critical heat current the counterflowing components become turbulent, forming a disordered tangle sustained by the relative motion between the two fluids, which is a driven-relative-motion instability with a critical onset threshold.
